Natural gas convertibility is standard on this unit, which aligns well with permanent outdoor kitchen installations where running a gas line is already part of the project.

The 30-inch, 40,000 BTU output is workable but not exceptional — buyers planning high-volume or commercial-adjacent use may find the BTU ceiling limiting compared to larger heads on the market. As a built-in head, it requires an existing or purpose-built island surround, so it is not an option for buyers who want a self-contained ready-to-use station. Installation complexity is higher than a freestanding grill, and that should be weighed against the seamless look it delivers. For a broader view of built-in and drop-in options in this category, Natural Gas Outdoor Grills — Best Picks, Reviews & Guides covers the full landscape.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

The 4+2 burner configuration with dedicated infrared side sear burners sets this Monument Grills unit apart from standard four-burner setups — infrared searing produces a noticeably better crust on steaks and chops than conventional burners at the same temperature. The Clearview lid lets you monitor food without lifting the lid and losing heat, a small but genuinely useful feature for longer cooks. Cabinet-style construction with knob controls and a built-in thermometer gives it a polished, integrated look at a $519 price point, which is the most accessible entry in this lineup. With 71 reviews at a perfect 5 out of 5, buyer satisfaction is clearly high for the price.

The propane-only listing means buyers committed to a natural gas line will need to verify conversion availability before purchasing, which is a non-trivial consideration for permanent outdoor kitchen builds. The blue colorway is distinctive but limits how easily it blends into neutral or stainless-dominant outdoor kitchen designs. At $519, some cost savings are likely reflected in the gauge and finish quality of the stainless steel compared to the 304-grade materials in the Brand-Man built-in head. Buyers interested in how this style fits into a larger outdoor kitchen context can compare options at Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

The Hygrill STD Series is marketed as heavy duty, and at $1,129.99 with 41 reviews at a perfect 5 out of 5, it has the ratings to back up that positioning for a built-in head in the premium tier. Natural gas is the native fuel here, making it a clean fit for anyone running a permanent gas line to an outdoor kitchen. As a built-in head, it prioritizes build quality and clean integration over add-on features, which suits buyers who want a long-lasting core grill component rather than a feature-loaded but thinner-gauge unit. The three-burner layout keeps the footprint compact relative to four- and five-burner heads, which is an advantage in medium-sized island builds.

Three burners will feel restrictive for hosts who regularly cook for large groups simultaneously, and at this price point, buyers should weigh whether the premium is justified versus the Brand-Man 30-inch head at $889.99 with a comparable rating. There are no BTU figures or additional feature specs available to further differentiate performance, which makes direct comparison harder than with more spec-transparent listings. Buyers researching the full range of natural gas built-in options will find additional context at Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026).

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

This Stanbroil unit is the only flat-top griddle in the group, which immediately distinguishes it from grate-based grills and makes it the right call for outdoor kitchen builds where smash burgers, breakfast cooks, stir-fries, and sautéed vegetables are on the regular menu. The 28-inch, 34,000 BTU, 2-burner configuration with a lid is well-suited as a complementary cooking zone alongside a primary grill head rather than as a standalone cooking solution. At $1,109.99 with 20 reviews averaging 4.5 out of 5, it positions as a considered premium purchase — the durable stainless steel and natural gas native design are consistent with permanent installation quality.

With only 20 reviews, the long-term performance data is thinner than for higher-volume products in this lineup, and the 4.5 rating, while good, suggests a small percentage of buyers encountered issues worth noting. As a griddle rather than a grill, it produces no grill marks and handles differently from a grate surface — buyers expecting dual-purpose performance from a single unit will be disappointed. The 28-inch footprint and 2-burner design also mean cooking capacity is inherently narrower than comparably priced grill heads. For buyers comparing grill heads and griddles as part of a full outdoor kitchen plan, Best Grills For Natural Gas (2026) provides a useful broader reference.

Buying guide

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a grill recovers heat and how efficiently it sears. For a freestanding island grill, 44,000–48,000 BTU across the main burners is a practical benchmark — the Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU on its five main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner island hits 44,000 BTU. A built-in head like the Brand-Man 30-inch runs 40,000 BTU, and the Hygrill STD 3-burner is positioned as heavy-duty without sacrificing that range. Raw BTU numbers only matter alongside cooking area, though: spreading 44,000 BTU across 864 sq. in. (as the Tytus does) produces lower surface heat density than the same output on a smaller grate, so always check both figures together.

Grate and body material determines long-term durability more than almost anything else. Porcelain-coated cast iron grates, like those on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and resist sticking but need careful maintenance to avoid chipping. Brushed or 304-grade stainless steel — specified on the Brand-Man built-in head and the Hygrill STD — is more forgiving in wet climates and easier to clean. For a flat-top format, the Stanbroil 28-inch griddle uses durable stainless steel with a lid, which is a meaningful addition because griddle surfaces corrode faster than grate surfaces when left exposed. If the grill will live in a permanent outdoor kitchen enclosure, a built-in head or built-in griddle is the right format; if you want flexibility, a freestanding island with storage cabinets gives you more options without requiring masonry work.

Fuel convertibility is worth examining before you buy. Several options here — including the Brand-Man 4-Burner island and the Brand-Man built-in head — are explicitly LPG/NG convertible, meaning you can run them on either propane or natural gas with the correct conversion kit. The Tytus Fresno is also listed as propane or natural gas compatible. If you're building a permanent outdoor kitchen connected to a gas line, confirm the model ships with or sells a natural gas conversion kit; for more on pairing grills with a built-in setup, see the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen guide. If you're still deciding between fuel types more broadly, the Natural Gas Outdoor Grills hub covers the tradeoffs in depth.

Extra burners and prep features have real cooking utility but also drive up footprint and price. Side burners — present on the Tytus, Brand-Man island, and Monument 4+2 — let you run sauces or sides without a separate cooktop. The Monument's infrared side sear burners are a distinct feature suited to finishing steaks at extreme temperatures. A fold-out prep table, like the one on the Brand-Man island, adds usable workspace without permanently enlarging the unit. Clearview lids (Monument) let you monitor cook progress without lifting and losing heat. Weigh these against your actual cooking habits: a flat-top griddle like the Stanbroil is the better choice for smash burgers and breakfast fare, but it does not replace a grate for traditional BBQ. Budget is the final filter — options here range from $519 (Monument) to $1,399.99 (Tytus), and the jump in price generally tracks with cooking area, burner count, and cabinet storage rather than raw BTU alone.

Related guides

If you're building out a full outdoor cooking setup, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchens guide covers built-in and drop-in options suited to permanent installations. For a broader look at fuel-specific picks, best barbeque grills natural gas compares top-rated natural gas models across price points and cooking styles. The natural gas outdoor grills hub ties everything together with category overviews and buying advice.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price range for a heavy-duty outdoor gas grill?

Among the options covered on this page, prices run from $519 for the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner cabinet-style grill up to $1,399.99 for the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill. Mid-range island stations with side burners and storage — like the Brand-Man 4-Burner at $799.99 — sit in the $800–$900 band. Built-in grill heads and griddles for outdoor kitchens typically start around $889 and reach $1,130 or more for premium stainless steel units. Budget roughly $500–$600 for a freestanding cabinet grill, and $900–$1,400 for a full island setup or high-end built-in head.

Which grills on this page can convert between propane and natural gas?

Several models here are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible, as is the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head (propane with natural gas conversion). The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill is sold as "Propane or Natural Gas." The Hygrill STD Series Built-In and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are listed as natural gas units. Always confirm that the conversion kit is included in the box or available separately before purchasing, especially if you plan to connect to a permanent gas line. For more natural gas–specific options, see the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) guide.

How do built-in grill heads differ from full island grill stations?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner or the Hygrill STD Series — is the cooking unit only. You drop it into a custom-built outdoor kitchen frame, concrete counter, or prefabricated island. A full island station such as the Tytus Fresno or Brand-Man 4-Burner Island arrives as a complete unit with cabinets, prep surfaces, and side burners already integrated. Built-in heads offer more flexibility for custom outdoor kitchens; island stations are faster to set up and better suited for patios without existing infrastructure. See the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) page for a deeper comparison.

Is a flat-top griddle better than a grated grill for outdoor cooking?

They serve different purposes. A flat-top griddle — like the Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle at 34,000 BTUs — excels at cooking eggs, smash burgers, vegetables, and anything that would fall through grates. Traditional grated burners produce char marks and allow fat to drip away, which many grillmasters prefer for steaks and bone-in cuts. The right choice depends on your primary cooking style. Some setups combine both: place a griddle on one end of an island and a grated grill head on the other.

How do BTU ratings translate to real cooking performance?

BTU figures measure raw heat output, not cooking quality. Higher BTUs heat a large cooking surface faster, which matters for searing and recovery time after opening the lid. The Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTUs across its main burners plus 12,000 on the side burner for a combined 60,000 BTUs — appropriate for its 864 sq. in. cooking surface. The Brand-Man Island station puts out 44,000 BTUs across four burners. As a rough guide, aim for 80–100 BTUs per square inch of primary cooking area. Monument's infrared side sear burners add high-intensity spot heat without inflating the main burner count. For the full picture on natural gas BTU requirements, check the Natural Gas Outdoor Grills guide.

What should buyers know about return policies and warranties before purchasing?

Return policies vary by retailer, not manufacturer. Before buying, confirm whether the seller accepts returns on large, heavy items and whether return shipping costs fall on the buyer — freight costs on a 200+ lb island grill can be substantial. On the warranty side, look for coverage on the stainless steel firebox and burners separately from cosmetic or surface components. 304 stainless steel construction — used in both Brand-Man built-in heads and the Hygrill STD Series — is a reliable indicator of durability, but it does not automatically extend warranty terms. Always read the warranty documentation before finalizing a purchase, particularly for built-in heads that will be cemented into a permanent outdoor kitchen structure.

This list is built for restaurateurs, caterers, and serious outdoor entertainers who need the output and build quality of the best commercial gas grills outdoor — units that can handle high-volume cooking, resist years of weather exposure, and run on natural gas or convert to it without compromise. Every pick was evaluated on BTU output, grate material, burner construction, and verified buyer feedback, with options spanning freestanding island setups to drop-in grill heads for built-in outdoor kitchen configurations. This is the value-leader in the commercial outdoor kitchen category, and it earns that position by delivering a complete, ready-to-use island setup at $799.99 — a price that undercuts most comparable stations by a significant margin. The 44,000 BTU output across four burners gives enough heat for batch cooking, catering prep, or a large household. The fold-out prep table and built-in storage cabinets make it genuinely functional as a standalone outdoor kitchen rather than just a grill on a cart. The LPG/NG convertibility is a practical bonus for anyone who wants flexibility or plans to plumb into a natural gas line later — a critical feature covered in more depth on the Natural Gas Outdoor Grills hub. With 113 reviews at 4.7/5, buyer satisfaction is unusually consistent for this price tier.

The black finish and overall construction lean toward the budget end of stainless work, so buyers expecting the same rigidity as a welded commercial kitchen unit will notice the difference. The side burner, while useful, is not rated with a dedicated BTU figure in the product specs, so high-heat wok or sauce work may feel limited. It's a strong all-rounder but not a professional-grade workhorse.

Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. Cooking, 48,000 BTU Main + 12,000 BTU Side Burner, Porcelain Grates, Storage Cabinets, Semi-Portable - GM/Ash Stone

The Tytus Fresno is built for buyers who want maximum cooking real estate in an island format. At 864 square inches of cooking surface, it can handle simultaneous zones for searing, indirect cooking, and holding — the kind of multi-zone flexibility that caterers and serious backyard entertainers need. The 48,000 BTU main burner output combined with a discrete 12,000 BTU side burner gives clear, useful specification for planning heat loads. Porcelain grates retain heat well and are easier to clean than bare cast iron. The semi-portable design is a genuine differentiator; most island grills are permanent installations, so the ability to reposition this unit has real utility. Dual-fuel compatibility (propane or natural gas) makes it adaptable for different setups, which is relevant to anyone comparing options across the best grills for natural gas category.

At $1,399.99 with only 16 reviews at 4.2/5, the confidence level in long-term durability is lower than it would be with a larger review pool. The GM/Ash Stone finish is distinctive but may not suit buyers who want a clean stainless aesthetic to match an existing outdoor kitchen. The price point also puts it in direct competition with more established brands that carry deeper review histories.

Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen

This is the right choice for anyone building or retrofitting a custom outdoor kitchen island who needs a drop-in grill head rather than a complete freestanding station. The 304 stainless steel construction is a meaningful spec — 304 is a marine-grade alloy that resists rust and heat stress better than the 430 stainless used in lower-cost units. At 40,000 BTU across four burners with natural gas convertibility, it covers serious cooking demands. The 5/5 rating across 72 reviews is a strong signal; that volume of feedback with a perfect score is rare and suggests consistent build quality and performance. Buyers planning a full outdoor kitchen setup should also compare this against options in the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen roundup for a broader view of the built-in segment.

At $889.99, it only covers the grill head — not a complete station — so total project cost will be higher once the surrounding cabinetry or island structure is factored in. The 30-inch width fits standard island cutouts but buyers with non-standard builds should verify dimensions before purchase. It also lacks a side burner, which rules it out for cooks who want supplementary heat zones in one unit.

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

The Hygrill STD Series is positioned squarely at the commercial and semi-commercial segment of the built-in grill head market. The "heavy duty" designation and $1,129.99 price point reflect construction intended for high-frequency use rather than occasional weekend grilling. It carries a 5/5 rating across 41 reviews — a substantial enough sample to treat as a reliable quality signal. For buyers building a permanent outdoor kitchen on natural gas who want a grill head that will hold up under daily or near-daily use, this is a credible option. Those evaluating it alongside other natural gas built-ins may find the best outdoor grills natural gas comparison useful context.

The three-burner configuration means total BTU output and cooking surface will be smaller than four- or five-burner alternatives at similar or lower price points. The product title does not specify a BTU rating, which makes direct heat-output comparisons difficult and is a gap buyers should address with the seller before purchase. At $1,129.99 for a grill head only, the price demands that the build quality genuinely outperform the competition — the reviews suggest it does, but the BTU ambiguity is a real drawback for buyers who plan their cooking zones by heat output.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

Monument's cabinet-style grill stands out for its feature density at $519. The 4+2 burner configuration — four main burners plus two infrared side sear burners alongside standard side burners — gives more cooking versatility than most grills in this price bracket. Infrared sear burners produce intense, direct radiant heat that exceeds what standard gas burners can achieve, making this relevant for anyone who wants steakhouse-quality sear marks. The Clearview® lid lets the cook monitor food without losing heat, and the built-in thermometer adds practical utility. At 5/5 across 71 reviews, buyer satisfaction is high. It's worth noting this is a propane unit; buyers specifically building around natural gas lines should review the best barbeque grills natural gas options before committing.

The blue color is a polarizing aesthetic choice that won't suit every outdoor setup, and unlike the built-in heads above, this is a freestanding cabinet unit rather than a drop-in for a custom island. No natural gas conversion is specified in the product data, which is a genuine limitation for buyers on a natural gas line. The lack of an explicit BTU total in the product title also makes heat-output comparisons harder to nail down without digging into the full spec sheet.

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

This is the only flat-top griddle in this roundup, which makes it genuinely distinct from the grate-based grills here. A 28-inch, 34,000 BTU two-burner natural gas griddle built into a BBQ island opens up a cooking style — smash burgers, stir-fry, breakfast service — that traditional grate grills can't replicate. The stainless steel flat top with a lid makes it weatherproof and island-ready without requiring additional covers or accessories. At $1,109.99 with a 4.5/5 rating across 20 reviews, the price is justified for anyone who specifically wants griddle cooking in a permanent outdoor kitchen installation. Natural gas readiness out of the box is a genuine convenience at this price point.

The 20-review count means the long-term durability picture is still forming, and at this price, buyers are taking on slightly more risk than they would with a product that has hundreds of reviews. Two burners and 28 inches of surface limits capacity — this is a supplemental station or a dedicated griddle zone within a larger outdoor kitchen, not a standalone solution for feeding a crowd. Anyone building a complete outdoor setup would likely need this alongside a separate grill rather than instead of one.

Buying guide

BTU output is the most cited spec in this category, but raw heat is only meaningful relative to cooking surface area. The Brand-Man 4-burner island pushes 44,000 BTU across its grate area, while the Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner — useful when you need a separate zone for sauces or a wok. The Stanbroil griddle runs 34,000 BTU across just two burners on a 28-inch flat top, which is a high BTU-per-square-inch ratio suited to searing and smash burgers. The practical question is whether you need high-output distributed heat (more burners, larger grate) or concentrated heat for a specific cooking style.

Construction grade separates weekend units from equipment that survives years of commercial-level use. Look for 304 stainless steel specifically — the Brand-Man built-in grill head calls this out explicitly, and it matters because 304 resists corrosion and pitting far better than lower-grade 430 stainless. Grate material is equally important: porcelain-coated cast iron, as found on the Tytus Fresno, retains heat evenly and releases food cleanly, but it chips if mishandled. For a built-in installation in an outdoor kitchen, a drop-in grill head like the Hygrill STD Series or the Brand-Man 30-inch head is the right format — these are engineered to be framed into masonry or stainless cabinetry rather than set on legs.

Fuel flexibility is a practical consideration if your setup might change. Several units here — the Brand-Man island, the Brand-Man built-in head, and the Tytus Fresno — are rated LPG/NG convertible, meaning they ship with the hardware to switch between propane and natural gas. If you are building a permanent outdoor kitchen with a dedicated natural gas line, conversion kits are straightforward to use; if you are on propane now but may add a gas line later, a convertible unit avoids replacing the grill entirely. For a deeper look at natural gas-specific options across all grill formats, see Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) and the broader Natural Gas Outdoor Grills guide.

Storage and prep space matter more than most buyers anticipate until they are mid-cook. The Brand-Man island, Tytus Fresno, and Monument Grills cabinet-style unit all include enclosed storage beneath the cooking surface — critical for keeping propane tanks, tools, and supplies organized in an outdoor setting exposed to weather. The Tytus Fresno adds 864 square inches of total cooking surface and a fold-out or extended prep configuration, making it viable for high-volume cooking. The Monument Grills unit stands out for including both infrared side sear burners and standard side burners at its price point, which gives you a wider range of cooking techniques without buying supplemental equipment. Price range across this group runs from $519 to $1,399.99, and the jump in cost generally tracks with cooking area, burner count, and the quality of cabinetry and grate materials rather than brand alone.

Related guides

For a broader look at the category, the best outdoor grills running on natural gas roundup compares freestanding and island-style models across a wider price range, making it a useful reference before committing to a commercial-grade unit.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum BTU output needed for a commercial-grade outdoor gas grill?

For serious outdoor cooking — whether for a restaurant patio, catering setup, or high-volume backyard use — look for a minimum of 40,000 BTUs across the main burners. Most of the grills on this page meet or exceed that threshold. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island delivers 44,000 BTU, the Tytus Fresno hits 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, and the Brand-Man Built-In Head puts out 40,000 BTU. Higher BTU alone doesn't guarantee better results — burner spacing, grate material, and lid seal all affect real-world heat retention — but anything under 30,000 BTU across four burners will struggle to recover temperature quickly during busy cooking sessions.

Which commercial gas grills are convertible between propane and natural gas?

Several options here are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is explicitly rated LPG/NG convertible, and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is listed as natural gas convertible from propane. The Tytus Fresno is available in propane or natural gas configurations. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle and the Hygrill STD Series are sold as dedicated natural gas units. If you're running a permanent outdoor kitchen with a natural gas line, built-in NG models are preferable — conversion kits can affect warranty coverage and, done incorrectly, create a safety hazard. For more natural gas-specific options, see the Best Grills For Natural Gas (2026) guide.

How do Brand-Man and Hygrill compare for a built-in outdoor kitchen installation?

Both brands offer built-in grill heads in heavy-gauge stainless steel, but they differ in price, burner count, and build spec. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head comes in at $889.99 with four burners and 40,000 BTU, using 304 stainless steel — the same marine-grade alloy used in professional kitchen equipment. The Hygrill STD Series sits at $1,129.99 as a 3-burner unit in natural gas only. Hygrill carries a 5/5 rating across 41 reviews and positions itself as a heavy-duty specialist product, which may justify the premium for buyers who want a purpose-built commercial grill head rather than a consumer unit adapted for built-in use. The Brand-Man also holds a 5/5 rating across 72 reviews, making it the stronger choice if BTU output and budget matter more than brand positioning.

What price range should buyers expect for commercial outdoor gas grills?

Entry points for genuinely heavy-duty outdoor grills start around $519 (Monument Grills 4+2 Burner), though that unit skews more toward prosumer than true commercial territory. Mid-tier commercial-style setups — full island configurations with storage, side burners, and convertible fuel — run $799 to $1,400, as seen with the Brand-Man Island at $799.99 and the Tytus Fresno at $1,399.99. Specialty built-in heads and griddles aimed at outdoor kitchen installations land between $889 and $1,130. True restaurant-specification equipment from commercial kitchen suppliers can run $3,000 and up, but the products on this page represent the upper tier of what's practical for high-volume outdoor cooking without requiring a commercial kitchen contractor.

Is a flat-top griddle a viable alternative to a traditional grate grill for outdoor commercial use?

Yes, depending on the menu. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Natural Gas Griddle provides a 34,000 BTU, 2-burner flat-top surface in durable stainless steel with a lid — suitable for smash burgers, breakfast service, stir-fry, or high-volume short-order cooking where grill marks aren't the priority. It earns a 4.5/5 across 20 reviews and integrates directly into a BBQ island. If the cooking program includes both grilling and flat-top work, consider pairing a griddle station with a traditional grate grill in a modular outdoor kitchen layout. For a broader look at outdoor kitchen grill options, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

What should buyers check before purchasing a commercial gas grill online — particularly around returns and warranty?

Before ordering any large outdoor grill online, confirm the retailer's freight return policy — these units are heavy and often ship via freight carrier, meaning standard return windows and free return shipping rarely apply. Check whether the manufacturer warranty covers commercial use; some residential warranty terms exclude commercial or semi-commercial operation. For built-in units like the Hygrill STD or Brand-Man Built-In Head, verify that converting fuel type (if applicable) doesn't void the warranty. Also confirm local code requirements for natural gas connections — most jurisdictions require a licensed plumber or gas fitter to make the final hookup, which adds to installation cost but is non-negotiable for safety and insurance purposes.

Buying guide

BTU output is the most-cited spec in this category, but raw heat is only meaningful relative to cooking surface area. The Brand-Man 4-burner island delivers 44,000 BTU across its grate, while the Tytus Fresno pushes 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a separate 12,000 BTU side burner — useful for sauces or a wok. The Monument Grills 4+2 unit adds infrared side sear burners on top of its standard array, giving you targeted high-heat zones without running the whole grill at full power. A figure around 90–100 BTU per square inch of cooking surface is a reasonable target; anything significantly below that and you'll struggle to recover temperature after loading a cold grate.

Construction grade separates grills that survive a season from ones built for a decade of outdoor use. Grade 304 stainless steel — explicitly called out in the Brand-Man Built-In Head at $889.99 and the Hygrill STD Series — resists chloride corrosion and surface pitting far better than 430-grade alternatives common in lower-cost units. Grate material matters too: porcelain-coated grates, as on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and clean easily, but chip over time; raw stainless or cast-iron grates are more durable with proper seasoning. If the grill will live in a permanent outdoor kitchen island, a built-in head format (the Brand-Man 30-inch or the Hygrill) is preferable to a freestanding unit because the surround structure handles weather exposure, leaving only the cooking deck exposed.

Fuel flexibility is worth scrutinizing before purchase. Several units here — the Brand-Man 4-burner island and the Brand-Man Built-In Head — are explicitly LPG/NG convertible, meaning a conversion kit lets you switch between propane and a natural gas line without buying a second grill. The Tytus Fresno is listed as propane or natural gas from the factory. If you're running a permanent outdoor kitchen on a natural gas line, confirm the unit ships with or supports an NG orifice; don't assume convertibility. The Stanbroil 28-inch griddle is spec'd specifically as natural gas, making it a clean drop-in for an NG island build without any conversion step. For a broader look at how these grills fit into permanent outdoor setups, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

Storage and prep infrastructure determines how functional the grill station actually is during service. The Brand-Man island and the Tytus Fresno both include storage cabinets; the Brand-Man adds a fold-out prep table, which matters if counter space around the grill is limited. The Monument Grills cabinet-style unit has an enclosed base as well, keeping fuel, tools, and accessories out of the weather. Built-in heads like the Hygrill and Brand-Man 30-inch are designed to drop into a custom surround, so storage depends entirely on how the island is built around them — factor that into your total cost. Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a commercial-grade outdoor gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a heavy-duty stainless steel unit with multiple burners. The Monument Grills 4+2-burner model starts at $519 and sits at the entry point of this tier. Purpose-built island grills and built-in heads with 304 stainless steel construction — like the Brand-Man built-in head at $889.99 or the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 — reflect the more typical price range for components that can handle sustained commercial or semi-commercial use. Full island stations with storage cabinets and side burners push into the $800–$1,400 range.

Which brands make the most reliable heavy-duty outdoor gas grills?

Among the options here, Brand-Man and Hygrill both carry perfect 5/5 ratings across dozens of verified reviews — Brand-Man's built-in head has 72 reviews at 5/5, and Hygrill's STD Series has 41 at 5/5. Tytus earns a solid 4.2/5 from 16 reviewers on its Fresno island grill. Monument Grills also holds a 5/5 across 71 reviews on its cabinet-style propane unit. For built-in griddle applications, Stanbroil is the specialist option at 4.5/5. The pattern across this category is that 304 stainless steel construction and heavy-gauge grates are the distinguishing durability factors, not brand name alone.

Can I convert a propane grill to natural gas, and which models support it?

Yes, but only if the manufacturer includes a conversion kit or explicitly rates the unit as dual-fuel. The Brand-Man 4-burner island grill ($799.99) and the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head ($889.99) are both listed as LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno island grill ($1,399.99) also supports both propane and natural gas. If you plan to run a permanent natural gas line, confirm the model you choose ships with a natural gas orifice kit or that one is available separately — not all convertible grills include it in the box. For more options in this category, see the full Natural Gas Outdoor Grills guide.

What BTU output should a commercial outdoor gas grill have?

For serious outdoor cooking — whether restaurant use, catering, or large-scale entertaining — aim for at least 10,000 BTU per main burner as a baseline. The Brand-Man island grill puts out 44,000 BTU across four burners, the Tytus Fresno delivers 48,000 BTU on the main grill plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, and the Brand-Man built-in head hits 40,000 BTU from four burners. Higher total BTU matters less than even heat distribution across the cooking surface; porcelain or cast stainless grates help retain and spread heat regardless of raw burner output.

How does a built-in grill head differ from a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-inch head ($889.99), the Hygrill STD Series ($1,129.99), or the Stanbroil griddle ($1,109.99) — is a drop-in cooking unit designed to be framed into a custom outdoor kitchen island. You supply the surrounding structure, countertop, and cabinetry. A freestanding island grill like the Brand-Man 4-burner station ($799.99) or the Tytus Fresno ($1,399.99) arrives as a complete unit with storage cabinets and a prep surface already integrated. Built-in heads give more design flexibility and typically use heavier commercial construction; island stations are faster to set up and move if needed. For outdoor kitchen-specific guidance, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

Are commercial outdoor gas grills worth it for home use?

If you cook outdoors regularly, host large gatherings, or want a permanent outdoor kitchen setup, the answer is yes. Commercial-grade 304 stainless steel resists rust and warping far better than consumer-grade materials, heavy-duty grates hold heat more consistently, and higher BTU burners recover temperature faster after food is loaded. The tradeoff is upfront cost and, for built-in heads, the need to build or buy an island structure. For buyers who want natural gas capability without a full island build, the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) page covers freestanding options across a wider price range.

Buying guide

BTU output matters, but only in relation to cooking surface area. A grill with 48,000 BTUs spread across 864 sq. in. (like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner) delivers a different heat density than one with 40,000 BTUs on a smaller 30-inch head (the Brand-Man Built-In Head). For open-flame grilling, aim for roughly 80–100 BTUs per square inch of primary cooking area — anything lower and searing performance suffers in cold or windy conditions. Side burners add flexibility for sauces and sides, but confirm they're independently controlled so they don't monopolize gas pressure from the main burners.

Construction grade separates grills that last five seasons from those that rust out in two. Full 304 stainless steel is the benchmark for built-in heads destined for a permanent outdoor kitchen — the Brand-Man Built-In Head and Hygrill STD Series both specify it. Porcelain-coated grates (as on the Tytus Fresno) retain heat well but can chip if mishandled; cast stainless grates are more forgiving for daily use. For island builds, also check cabinet material: stone veneer finishes like the Tytus Fresno's GM/Ash Stone handle weather better than powder-coated steel panels over time.

Fuel flexibility is worth weighing early. Several options here — including the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Brand-Man Built-In Head — are LPG/NG convertible, which means you can start with a propane tank and switch to a natural gas line later without buying a new grill. If you're building a permanent outdoor kitchen with a dedicated gas line from the start, a natural-gas-only unit like the Hygrill STD Series or the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle removes a conversion step and is often plumbed more cleanly. For more on pairing grills with permanent structures, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

Format — freestanding island vs. built-in drop-in head — determines installation complexity and long-term flexibility. A drop-in head like the Hygrill STD Series ($1,129.99) requires a surrounding structure but integrates seamlessly into custom builds. Frequently asked questions

What is a realistic budget for a heavy-duty outdoor gas grill?

Entry-level island-style grill stations start around $520 — the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner Cabinet Style lands at $519 and includes infrared side sear burners and a Clearview lid. Mid-range built-in heads and full grill islands run $800–$900, as seen with the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 and the Brand-Man 30-inch Built-In Head at $889.99. Premium built-in options with heavy-gauge stainless construction — like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner at $1,129.99 or the Stanbroil 28-inch Natural Gas Griddle at $1,109.99 — sit above $1,100. Full 5-burner propane/NG island packages, such as the Tytus Fresno at $1,399.99, represent the top of the range covered here.

Which grills can run on natural gas, and which are propane only?

Several models here are dual-fuel ready. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Island and the Brand-Man 30-inch Built-In Head are both listed as LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island is sold as a propane or natural gas unit. The Hygrill STD Series Built-In Grill Head is listed specifically as natural gas. The Stanbroil 28-inch Griddle is a natural gas model. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner is listed as a propane gas grill — confirm with the manufacturer before connecting to a natural gas line. For a deeper look at NG-specific options, see the Natural Gas Outdoor Grills guide.

How does BTU output actually affect cooking performance?

BTU ratings measure heat input, not cooking performance in isolation — grate material, lid design, and cooking area all matter equally. That said, as a rough benchmark, 100 BTUs per square inch of primary cooking surface is a reasonable minimum for effective searing. The Tytus Fresno puts out 48,000 BTUs across 864 sq. in. of cooking area, which works out to about 55 BTUs per sq. in. — sufficient for general grilling but not aggressive searing. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island delivers 44,000 BTUs, while the Brand-Man Built-In Head produces 40,000 BTUs across a 30-inch format. The Stanbroil griddle generates 34,000 BTUs across a flat-top surface where even, moderate heat is preferable to concentrated high heat.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a full grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-Burner or the Hygrill STD Series — is the cooking unit only, designed to drop into a custom or prefabricated outdoor kitchen frame. You supply the surrounding cabinetry, countertop, and storage. A full grill island — like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island Station or the Tytus Fresno — comes with integrated storage cabinets, side burners, and a prep surface already assembled. Islands cost less than custom builds and suit buyers who want an out-of-the-box outdoor kitchen setup. Built-in heads are the right choice when integrating into an existing or custom-designed outdoor kitchen. See also Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen for built-in-focused comparisons.

Is a flat-top griddle a good alternative to a traditional grate grill for outdoor use?

It depends entirely on what you cook most. A flat-top like the Stanbroil 28-inch Natural Gas Griddle excels at foods that benefit from full surface contact — smash burgers, breakfast items, fish fillets, vegetables — and eliminates flare-up risk. A traditional grate grill is better for charring, open-flame flavor, and cooking large cuts with drip-induced smoke. Many outdoor kitchens incorporate both. At $1,109.99, the Stanbroil is a serious investment for griddle-only use, so consider whether a dedicated flat-top makes sense alongside another grate-based unit or replaces one.

Which option makes the most sense for a first outdoor kitchen build?

The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Island at $799.99 is the most practical starting point for most buyers building a first outdoor kitchen. It carries a 4.7/5 rating across 113 reviews — the highest review count in this group — and includes storage cabinets, a fold-out prep table, a side burner, and LPG/NG convertibility in one package. Buyers who want more BTUs and cooking area and can stretch the budget should look at the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner at $1,399.99, which adds 864 sq. in. of cooking space and 48,000 BTUs of main-burner output. title: Best Gas Outdoor Grills (2026)
metaDescription: Best Gas Outdoor Grills (2026). Editor's picks, in-depth review, and comparisons for "gas outdoor grills".
outline:
Disclosure: This page contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you purchase through them — at no extra cost to you. Recommendations are based on independent testing and editorial judgment.

This guide to the best gas outdoor grills covers everything from freestanding cabinet-style units to built-in island heads, chosen for buyers who want serious output, durable construction, and long-term reliability rather than entry-level weekend hardware. Each pick was evaluated on BTU output, grate material, build quality, and dual-fuel flexibility — the factors that separate grills worth owning from grills worth replacing. Whether the priority is a full outdoor kitchen setup or a standalone station that holds its own through years of heavy use, the options below represent the strongest performers across those criteria.

Comparison table

Use the table below to size up the six picks at a glance before diving into full reviews.

Product Price Rating Standout
Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner (44,000 BTU, LPG/NG Convertible) $799.99 4.7/5 (113 reviews) Value
Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill (864 sq. in., 48,000 BTU + 12,000 BTU Side Burner) $1,399.99 4.2/5 (16 reviews) Capacity

Stanbroil 28-Inch 2-Burner Built-In Natural Gas Griddle (Flat Top, 34,000 BTU, Stainless Steel) $1,109.99 4.5/5 (20 reviews) Griddle

Top picks

Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner - 44,000 BTU Stainless Steel BBQ Grill Station with Storage Cabinets, Fold-out Prep Table, LPG/NG Convertible, Black

This grill station is built for the backyard cook who wants a complete outdoor kitchen setup without spending four figures. The 44,000 BTU output across four burners delivers serious heat for large cooks, and the fold-out prep table plus storage cabinets mean everything you need stays within arm's reach. LPG/NG convertibility is a genuine practical advantage — switching fuel sources doesn't require buying a new unit. At $799.99 with a 4.7/5 rating from 113 reviewers, it earns broad confidence across a wide buyer pool.

The stainless steel finish and island cabinet construction look the part, but buyers comparing this to higher-end built-in heads should note this is a freestanding station, not a drop-in unit. Those integrating into a masonry or tile outdoor kitchen will find the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head a more appropriate fit. The black cabinet finish may also show heat discoloration over time in high-use environments.

Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. Cooking, 48,000 BTU Main + 12,000 BTU Side Burner, Porcelain Grates, Storage Cabinets, Semi-Portable - GM/Ash Stone

The Tytus Fresno targets serious grillers who prioritize cooking area above all else. An 864 sq. in. total cooking surface paired with 48,000 BTU from the main burners makes it one of the most capable island grills in this price range, and the dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner adds genuine multi-task versatility. Porcelain grates retain heat well and resist rust better than bare cast iron, which matters for a unit left outdoors year-round. The semi-portable design gives it flexibility most built-in islands can't match.

At $1,399.99, the Tytus Fresno is the most expensive freestanding unit here, and its 4.2/5 rating from just 16 reviews means confidence in long-term reliability is thinner than competitors with larger review samples. Buyers who need maximum cook area will find it compelling, but those on tighter budgets who don't need the fifth burner or the extra square footage should look hard at the Brand-Man island station at $600 less.

Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen

This is the right choice for anyone building a permanent outdoor kitchen who needs a drop-in grill head rather than a freestanding station. The 304 stainless steel construction is a meaningful spec upgrade — 304 grade resists corrosion and surface pitting significantly better than lower-grade stainless, which matters when a unit is embedded in a fixed island exposed to weather. At 40,000 BTU across four burners and natural gas convertible, it pairs well with a permanent gas line. The 5/5 rating from 72 reviews is the strongest confidence signal in this entire roundup.

At $889.99, the price is fair for a 304-grade built-in head, but buyers must already have or plan to build a compatible outdoor kitchen island — this unit ships without cabinet storage or prep surfaces. Those who haven't yet committed to a fixed outdoor kitchen structure should consider the Brand-Man island station instead, which bundles storage and a prep table at a lower price point.

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

The Hygrill STD is positioned as a heavy-duty built-in option for natural gas outdoor kitchens, and its 5/5 rating from 41 reviewers backs that reputation with meaningful sample size. Three burners suit smaller outdoor kitchen footprints or buyers who grill frequently for two to four people rather than large crowds. The "heavy duty" designation and stainless construction suggest this is built to last in a fixed installation, making it a credible long-term investment for a permanent kitchen island.

At $1,129.99, the Hygrill STD is priced above the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head despite offering one fewer burner and 40,000 BTU versus the Brand-Man's stated 40,000 BTU output — buyers will want to weigh whether the Hygrill's build quality justifies the premium. It is also a built-in head only, so it requires an existing or planned island structure. Those who want more burners per dollar should compare this directly against the Brand-Man built-in option before purchasing.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

At $519, the Monument Grills 4+2 burner is the entry point for buyers who want a full-featured cabinet-style gas grill without committing to an outdoor kitchen build. The configuration is unusually generous at this price: four main burners plus two infrared side sear burners and two side burners give serious flexibility for simultaneous cooking tasks. The Clearview® lid and built-in thermometer add practical convenience for monitoring cooks without lifting the lid. Its 5/5 rating from 71 reviews reflects strong satisfaction across a solid reviewer base.

This is a propane-only unit, which limits placement options for buyers who have or plan to run a natural gas line. The blue cabinet finish stands out visually, which is a style preference that may not suit every patio aesthetic. Cabinet-style freestanding grills in this category also typically use lighter-gauge stainless than built-in heads constructed from 304 grade material, so longevity in harsh climates may be a consideration for buyers planning to leave it outdoors year-round.

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

The Stanbroil griddle fills a specific gap that none of the other products here address: flat-top cooking in a built-in format. For outdoor kitchen builders who want smash burgers, breakfast foods, stir-fry, or any application that benefits from a flat cooking surface, this is the only purpose-built option in the roundup. At 34,000 BTU across two burners and 28 inches wide, it delivers enough heat for a productive flat-top cook without overbuilding for occasional use. The 4.5/5 rating from 20 reviewers reflects early but positive reception.

At $1,109.99, the Stanbroil is priced near the Hygrill STD built-in grill head, so buyers must be clear they want griddle-style cooking rather than grate-based grilling before committing. The lid is included, which helps protect the flat surface between cooks. With only 20 reviews, the long-term durability picture is less established than competitors — buyers who want a flat-top but value a deeper review record may want to monitor this model's feedback over the next season before purchasing. For a broader look at built-in options suited to permanent outdoor kitchens, see the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) guide.

Buying guide

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a grill can reach searing temperatures, but raw numbers need context. Among the grills on this page, main-burner output runs from 34,000 BTU (Stanbroil 28-Inch griddle, 2 burners) up to 48,000 BTU on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island. Higher BTU matters most for large, frequent cooks — a 44,000 BTU, 4-burner setup like the Brand-Man Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island covers serious volume without wasting fuel on a quick weeknight dinner. For pure searing, check whether a model adds an infrared side sear burner; the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner includes two infrared side sear burners specifically for that purpose.

Cooking surface area directly limits how much food you can run at once. The Tytus Fresno leads this group at 864 sq. in., which suits large gatherings. Built-in grill heads like the Brand-Man 30-Inch (4-burner, 40,000 BTU) and the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner are sized to drop into an outdoor kitchen frame rather than stand alone, so measure your island cutout before buying — a 30-inch head fits a different opening than a 28-inch griddle. If the plan is flat-top cooking rather than grate grilling, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle is the only dedicated flat-top option in this lineup and accepts natural gas natively.

Fuel flexibility matters if your utility hookup might change or if you want one grill that works at two properties. The Brand-Man Kitchen Grill Island and Brand-Man Built-In Head are both LPG/NG convertible, while the Tytus Fresno explicitly supports propane or natural gas. The Hygrill STD Series, by contrast, is listed as natural gas only, so confirm your supply before ordering. Built-in thermometers (Monument Grills) and clearview lids reduce guesswork during longer cooks without lifting the lid and dropping temperature.

Storage and prep infrastructure determine whether the grill becomes a true outdoor kitchen station or just a cooking appliance. The Brand-Man Island and Tytus Fresno both include storage cabinets; the Brand-Man adds a fold-out prep table, which matters when counter space is limited. Built-in heads (Brand-Man 30-Inch, Hygrill, Stanbroil) ship without cabinetry because they assume an existing island — factor in the cost of that surrounding structure. Material quality anchors long-term durability: the Brand-Man 30-Inch specifies heavy-duty 304 stainless steel, the benchmark for corrosion resistance in outdoor cooking equipment exposed to weather year-round.

Related guides

Building a full outdoor kitchen setup? The best gas grills for outdoor kitchens guide covers drop-in heads, built-in configurations, and island-ready options in detail. For a broader look at fuel-specific picks, the best outdoor grills running on natural gas roundup compares top-rated models across size and budget categories.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum BTU output worth considering for a heavy-duty outdoor gas grill?

For a full-size outdoor grill, 40,000 BTU across the main burners is a reasonable floor. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island delivers 44,000 BTU, which is enough to maintain high searing temperatures across a large cooking surface. Anything below 30,000 BTU on a multi-burner unit will struggle with high-heat searing or cooking for a crowd. Side burners are rated separately — the Tytus Fresno adds a 12,000 BTU side burner on top of its 48,000 BTU main output, which is a meaningful bonus for sauces or side dishes.

Can I convert a propane gas grill to natural gas?

Several models on this list are factory-built for dual-fuel flexibility. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head (30-inch, 4-burner) are both explicitly LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is also available in propane or natural gas configuration. Conversion typically requires a separate kit and, in most cases, a licensed gas technician to make the connection safely — check local code before switching fuels.

What is the price difference between a freestanding grill and a built-in grill head?

Freestanding island units like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island start around $799.99 and include cabinets, a prep table, and a complete frame. Built-in grill heads designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen counter — such as the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head at $889.99 or the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 — cost more per unit but require a separately constructed island. Factor in cabinet and countertop costs when comparing total project spend.

Are stainless steel grates better than porcelain-coated grates?

Both materials appear across these grills. Bare stainless steel grates, like those on the Hygrill STD Series, are durable and easy to restore with cleaning but require seasoning to prevent sticking. Porcelain-coated grates, as found on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat evenly and resist rust as long as the coating is intact — chips or cracks expose the underlying metal to corrosion. For high-use commercial-style setups, heavy-gauge stainless grates tend to have a longer service life.

How much cooking area do I need for feeding a large group?

A general benchmark is 72–100 square inches of cooking surface per person for a seated dinner. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner offers 864 sq. in. of total cooking area, which is one of the largest on this list and comfortably handles 8–12 people at once. For smaller gatherings of 4–6, a 4-burner unit with around 600–700 sq. in. is typically sufficient. Check both primary grate area and warming rack dimensions, since manufacturers often combine both figures to reach a total square-inch number.

Is a built-in flat-top griddle worth considering alongside a traditional grill?

A dedicated flat-top griddle handles foods that fall through grill grates — eggs, diced vegetables, smash burgers, seafood — and does so with more even heat distribution than a cast-iron griddle insert. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle puts out 34,000 BTU across two burners and is designed specifically to drop into an outdoor kitchen island alongside a separate grill head. If the outdoor kitchen will see diverse cooking beyond standard grilling, pairing a griddle with a traditional burner setup is a practical upgrade rather than a luxury.

title: Best Outdoor Gas Grills (2026)

Disclosure: This page contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you purchase through them — at no extra cost to you. Recommendations are based on independent testing and editorial judgment.

This list of the best outdoor gas grills is built for buyers who want serious cooking performance — whether that means a freestanding island grill for backyard entertaining or a built-in head for a permanent outdoor kitchen setup. Every pick was evaluated on BTU output, cooking area, build quality, and verified buyer feedback, with a hard focus on models that hold up under regular heavy use. Propane-only rigs, convertible NG units, and dedicated natural gas grills are all represented, so there's a fit regardless of your fuel situation.

Comparison table

All six grills at a glance — sorted by price, lowest to highest.

Grill Price Rating Standout Strength
Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grill $519 5/5 (71 reviews) Value
Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island $799.99 4.7/5 (113 reviews) Popularity

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In Grill $1,129.99 5/5 (41 reviews) Durability
Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill $1,399.99 4.2/5 (16 reviews) Capacity

Top picks

Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. Cooking, 48,000 BTU Main + 12,000 BTU Side Burner, Porcelain Grates, Storage Cabinets, Semi-Portable - GM/Ash Stone

Built for serious backyard cooks who want island-style functionality without a permanent installation, the Tytus Fresno delivers 864 square inches of cooking space across five burners pushing 48,000 BTU, plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner for sauces or sides. The semi-portable design and dual-fuel compatibility (propane or natural gas) make it unusually flexible for a grill at this size, and the porcelain grates and built-in storage cabinets give it the polish of a fixed outdoor kitchen. At $1,399.99 with a 4.2/5 rating across 16 reviews, it earns its price for buyers who want capacity and convenience in one unit.

The trade-off is that 16 reviews is a thin sample, so long-term durability data is limited compared to more established models. The GM/Ash Stone finish is distinctive but polarizing, and buyers who prefer a clean stainless look will need to look elsewhere. Semi-portable also means it won't satisfy those who want a fully fixed, drop-in installation.

Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner - 44,000 BTU Stainless Steel BBQ Grill Station with Storage Cabinets, Fold-out Prep Table, LPG/NG Convertible, Black

The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island is the best-reviewed complete grill station on this list, earning 4.7/5 from 113 reviews — a sample large enough to trust. At $799.99, it undercuts the Tytus Fresno by $600 while still delivering 44,000 BTU, a side burner, storage cabinets, and a fold-out prep table that adds meaningful workspace. LPG/NG convertibility means buyers aren't locked into one fuel source, and the black stainless finish suits modern outdoor kitchen builds.

Four burners at 44,000 BTU total means less output per burner than some competitors, which can matter when searing large cuts or running the grill at full capacity. The all-black aesthetic, while sharp, may show heat discoloration more visibly over time. It's a complete station rather than a built-in head, so buyers planning a fully integrated masonry kitchen will want a different format.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

Monument's 4+2 burner configuration stands out for including infrared side sear burners alongside standard burners — a combination that gives serious grillers high-heat searing capability without buying a separate unit. The Clearview® lid, built-in thermometer, and cabinet-style base make it a well-equipped freestanding option, and at $519 it's the most affordable full-featured grill on this list. A perfect 5/5 from 71 reviews is a strong signal of consistent buyer satisfaction.

This is a propane-only model, which limits it for buyers specifically shopping for a natural gas connection. The blue color finish, while unique, is not a neutral choice and won't suit every patio aesthetic. Cabinet-style bases also tend to offer less versatile storage than purpose-built island configurations with proper cabinet doors and drawers.

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

Buyers building a custom outdoor kitchen who need a drop-in grill head rather than a freestanding station should look closely at the Hygrill STD Series. It's a dedicated natural gas built-in head — no conversion kit required — rated 5/5 from 41 reviews at $1,129.99. The heavy-duty stainless steel construction is built for a permanent installation where the surrounding structure handles storage and prep surface, making it a clean fit for masonry or cabinetry builds.

Three burners is a step down from the four- and five-burner competition at similar price points, so total cooking capacity is more limited. At $1,129.99 for a grill head alone — no island, no side burner, no prep table — the per-feature cost is high unless a custom build genuinely calls for this format. Buyers who aren't constructing a dedicated outdoor kitchen island will find freestanding options a better value.

Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen

For built-in buyers who want more burners than the Hygrill STD Series without stepping up to the Tytus price point, the Brand-Man 30-inch built-in head offers four burners and 40,000 BTU in heavy-duty 304 stainless steel — a grade well-suited for permanent outdoor exposure. Natural gas convertibility is included, and the perfect 5/5 rating from 72 reviews at $889.99 gives it a stronger credibility base than many grill heads in this segment.

As a built-in head, it shares the same limitation as the Hygrill: no storage, no side burner, no prep surface unless the surrounding island provides them. Buyers who haven't planned that infrastructure will find the freestanding island-style grills more immediately usable. Propane is the default configuration, so natural gas buyers will need to complete the conversion before the first cook.

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

The Stanbroil 28-inch griddle is the only flat-top option on this list, making it the right pick for outdoor kitchens that prioritize smash burgers, breakfast cooking, stir-fry, or anything requiring a continuous cooking surface over open grates. It runs on natural gas natively, outputs 34,000 BTU across two burners, and includes a lid — a detail that matters for heat retention and weather protection. At $1,109.99 with a 4.5/5 from 20 reviews, it fills a specific niche that no other product here addresses.

A 2-burner flat-top griddle is a complement to a grill, not a replacement for one — buyers who want traditional grate cooking will need a separate unit. The 20-review sample is modest, and the price is high for a 28-inch two-burner surface. It's purpose-built for outdoor kitchen islands; anyone without a proper built-in cutout and a natural gas line already run will find it difficult to put to use. For more options in this category, see the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) guide.

Buying guide

BTU output and cooking area are the two numbers that matter most, but neither means much without the other. A grill pumping 48,000 BTUs across 864 sq. in. of cooking surface — like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner — can sustain high heat across a large cook without crowding. A smaller unit such as the Hygrill STD 3-Burner built-in delivers focused output for a dedicated island slot where raw square footage matters less than consistent, even heat. Match BTU-to-area ratio to how you actually cook: high-volume cooks need both, occasional weekend grilling can get by with less.

Fuel flexibility separates versatile grills from permanent installations. Several options here — including the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head — are LPG/NG convertible, which means you can run them on propane now and switch to a natural gas line later without replacing the unit. If you're already piping in natural gas, a dedicated NG grill like the Hygrill STD or the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle locks in that convenience permanently. Buyers planning a full outdoor kitchen should also check whether the unit ships as a drop-in grill head or a freestanding island with cabinetry — those are fundamentally different purchases. For more on integrating grills into a permanent setup, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

Cooking surface type is worth deliberate attention. Traditional grate-style grills with porcelain or stainless grates (the Tytus Fresno uses porcelain) suit classic grilling, while a flat-top griddle like the Stanbroil 28-Inch opens up smash burgers, seafood, and vegetables without flare-up risk. Infrared side sear burners — found on the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner — add steakhouse-level searing capability without upgrading the whole unit. Think about which cooking methods you'll actually use before defaulting to the highest BTU count.

Build quality and reviewer confidence should carry real weight. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head is constructed from heavy-duty 304 stainless steel and holds a 5/5 rating across 72 reviews — that combination of material spec and sustained buyer satisfaction is a meaningful signal. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner and Hygrill STD both hold 5/5 ratings as well, with 71 and 41 reviews respectively. Contrast that with a lower price point and fewer reviews, and you have a clearer picture of where the proven value actually sits. Explore the full natural gas category at Natural Gas Outdoor Grills — Best Picks, Reviews & Guides if you want to compare beyond this shortlist.

Related guides

If you're building a full outdoor setup, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchens guide covers island-ready and built-in options in depth. Shoppers committed to natural gas should also check the best outdoor grills for natural gas roundup, which focuses specifically on NG-native and convertible models across a wider price range.

Frequently asked questions

What is a reasonable budget for a heavy-duty outdoor gas grill in 2026?

Capable heavy-duty outdoor gas grills start around $519 — the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner hits that price point with stainless steel construction, infrared side sear burners, and a clearview lid. Mid-tier island-style setups with storage cabinets and fold-out prep tables sit in the $800–$900 range, while premium built-in grill heads and full island systems with 48,000 BTU outputs climb to $1,100–$1,400. Expect to pay more as you move from freestanding units to true built-in heads designed for permanent outdoor kitchen installations.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head — is a drop-in unit designed to be set into a custom outdoor kitchen counter or BBQ island frame. It has no legs or cabinet base of its own. A freestanding island grill, such as the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner or the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island, comes with its own cabinet structure, storage, and sometimes a prep table, so it can be positioned anywhere on a patio without additional construction.

Can these gas grills run on natural gas, or are they propane only?

Most models on this list support both. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head are both explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island and the Hygrill STD Series Built-In Grill are also offered in natural gas configurations. If you have a dedicated natural gas line, confirm the specific SKU or conversion kit is included before purchasing — some manufacturers ship a propane-configured unit with a separate NG orifice kit. For a deeper look at natural gas-specific models, see the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas (2026) guide.

How much cooking surface do I need for a large gathering?

A general rule: plan for roughly 72 square inches of primary cooking surface per person for a relaxed cookout pace. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner tops this list at 864 sq. in. of total cooking area, which comfortably handles 10–12 people cooking simultaneously. The Brand-Man 4-Burner island and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner both offer substantial main grates as well. For outdoor kitchens where a flat-top griddle is preferred over grates, the Stanbroil 28-Inch 2-Burner Built-In Griddle provides a wide flat cooking surface suited for batch cooking breakfast items, smash burgers, or stir-fry-style meals alongside a separate grill station.

Are stainless steel grates better than porcelain-coated grates?

Both have trade-offs. Bare stainless steel grates require seasoning but handle high heat without the risk of chipping, and they are easier to repair over time. Porcelain-coated grates — as found on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner — offer a non-stick-like surface that is easier to clean when new, but porcelain can crack or chip if scrubbed aggressively or subjected to thermal shock, and once the coating is compromised the underlying metal can rust. For heavy, frequent use in a permanent outdoor kitchen, many buyers prefer heavy-gauge stainless grates. For occasional use where easy cleanup is the priority, porcelain holds up well.

Is a side burner worth the extra cost on an outdoor gas grill?

A side burner earns its place if the grill is your primary outdoor cooking station — it handles sauces, corn, baked beans, or anything that needs a pot or pan without forcing you back inside. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner includes a 12,000 BTU side burner, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island includes one as well, and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner adds both infrared side sear burners and standard side burners. If space is limited and you are speccing a built-in head only, a side burner can always be added as a separate drop-in module in most outdoor kitchen island builds. metaTitle: Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Cooking (2026)
metaDescription: Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Cooking (2026). Editor's picks, in-depth review, and comparisons for "gas grills for outdoor cooking".
outline:

This guide to the best gas grills for outdoor cooking covers freestanding island units, built-in grill heads, and flat-top griddles — chosen to match the full range of outdoor setups, from backyard patios to fully equipped outdoor kitchens. Each pick was evaluated on BTU output, construction quality, cooking area, and verified buyer ratings. Whether the priority is a high-capacity natural gas island or a drop-in stainless grill head, the options below represent the strongest performers across price points currently available.
